name: edge-ssr-auth-header
description:
Shared-secret header between SSR origin (Vercel / Netlify / etc) and CDN edge (Cloudflare / AWS WAF / Fastly), used to let SSR fetches skip CDN bot-mitigation that would otherwise challenge them.

Why you want this: Next.js SSR functions make API fetches from generic node-fetch UAs on shared CDN egress IPs. CDN bot management routinely challenges this traffic, surfacing as HTTP 000 / dropped connections / silent 4xx for the SSR — which then becomes a visible 404 (or fallback shell) for the end user. This integration ships the SSR-side helper so requests carry an identifying header; you configure your CDN's WAF to skip challenges when the header matches a shared secret.

Real incident this pattern came from: a Next.js + Cloudflare-fronted EC2 API setup (2026-05). Customer-facing tenant pages 404'd intermittently because Cloudflare bot-challenged the SSR. The fix: this header + a Cloudflare WAF Skip rule. Full post-mortem in docs/edge-ssr-auth-header.md.

Opt-in by design. Only adds value when (1) you have an API behind a bot-managed CDN AND (2) SSR is the primary entry point for that API. If you're using Vercel + Neon (no CDN in front of Neon), skip this integration.
post_install_steps: Set INTERNAL_API_TOKEN as a SERVER-SIDE env var on your hosting platform (Vercel: Project Settings → Environment Variables; do NOT prefix NEXT_PUBLIC_, do NOT enable for browser exposure). Scopes: Production + Preview.

Configure your CDN's WAF rule to skip bot-mitigation when `http.request.headers["x-internal-token"]` matches the same secret. Click-path + curl examples for Cloudflare are in docs/edge-ssr-auth-header.md.
Verify: run a tight curl loop against a known-real URL that exercises SSR; expect 100% HTTP 200 instead of intermittent 4xx / fallback shells.

Wire the helper into SSR data fetchers: `const headers = getInternalApiHeaders(); await fetch(url, { headers });` — see the docstring inside the helper for the rationale + the call sites you should cover.

verification.dev: node -e "const m = require('./src/lib/internal-api-headers'); if (typeof m.getInternalApiHeaders !== 'function') { throw new Error('helper missing'); } console.log('edge-ssr-auth-header ok');"
